description: DCGM Xid errors and GPU health check failures across the cluster. Xid errors indicate GPU hardware or driver issues — see https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/xid-errors/ for error code reference. Data sourced from Crusoe Metrics (Watch Agent provides DCGM metrics; no separate DCGM exporter installation is required). | GPU Health section added: SBE/DBE counters, remapped rows, PCIe replay, tensor-core utilization. Designed for slow-node detection during training runs.

panels.description: Distinct GPUs that fired any non-zero Xid event in the last 24h.
panels.title: GPUs With Xid Events (Last 24h)

panels.description: Cluster-wide increase in uncorrectable (double-bit) ECC errors in the last 24h. Should normally be 0 — DBEs are unrecoverable.
panels.title: Total ECC Errors (Last 24h)

panels.description: Most recent Xid code per node, sustained via max_over_time so a momentary event stays visible for the rest of the window. 0 = healthy; non-zero values are the Xid code. Reference: https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/xid-errors/
panels.title: Xid Code by Node Over Time

panels.description: GPUs with any non-zero Xid in the last 24h, broken down by node, GPU, and Xid code. Xid code reference: https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/xid-errors/
panels.title: Xid Events by Node / GPU / Code (Last 24h)

panels.description: GPUs with new uncorrectable ECC events or any uncorrectable remapped HBM rows in the last 24h. Both warrant urgent hardware investigation.
panels.title: DCGM Health Failures (Last 24h)
GPU Health Indicators — Slow Node Detection
Active SBE GPUs
panels.description: GPUs currently reporting non-zero volatile single-bit ECC corrections. Means actively correcting since last reset — usually benign on its own.

panels.title: GPUs With Remapped Rows
panels.description: GPUs with at least one correctable HBM row remapped. Growing count over time is the classic 'HBM degrading' signal.

panels.title: Uncorrectable Rows
panels.description: Cluster-wide count of permanently uncorrectable HBM rows. Should be 0; any non-zero value means at least one GPU has unfixable HBM.

panels.title: 24h PCIe Replays
panels.description: Cluster-wide PCIe replay-counter increase in 24h. Non-zero indicates physical-layer link issues — bus glitches, marginal connectors, or a failing bridge.

panels.title: 24h SBE Volatile Δ
panels.description: Cluster-wide volatile single-bit ECC correction increase in 24h. Dozens to low thousands per GPU under sustained load is normal; runaway growth on one node is a slow-node candidate.

panels.title: Lifetime SBE (Σ)
panels.description: Fleet-wide lifetime aggregate of single-bit ECC corrections. Reference number only — distribution matters; see leaderboard below.

panels.title: Top 15 GPUs by Lifetime Aggregate SBE
panels.description: Lifetime single-bit ECC corrections per GPU. Useful for draining decisions or excluding from latency-critical jobs.

panels.title: Top 15 GPUs by Correctable Remapped Rows
panels.description: GPUs with HBM rows already retired. Still functional but with slightly less HBM; remap-region accesses can be marginally slower.

panels.title: Top 15 GPUs by 24h SBE Volatile Growth
panels.description: GPUs correcting hardest right now — 24h delta of the volatile single-bit ECC counter. Start here when chasing a same-day slow-node symptom.

panels.title: Red Flag GPUs (Uncorrectable Rows or PCIe Replays in 24h)
panels.description: GPUs with an uncorrectable remapped HBM row OR a non-zero 24h PCIe replay increase. Either condition warrants pulling the node from training for hardware investigation.

panels.title: SBE Volatile Rate by Node (last 6h)
panels.description: Per-GPU single-bit ECC correction rate, top 20 over 6h. Persistent elevation on one GPU = slow-node candidate; correlated bursts across many = HBM-stressing workload.

panels.title: Tensor Core Utilization by Node (last 1h)
panels.description: Tensor-core utilization per node, averaged across that node's GPUs. A node consistently below its peers during steady-state training is dragging the collective.

panels.title: Top GPUs by Lifetime Uncorrectable ECC (DBE)
panels.description: Lifetime double-bit ECC error count per GPU. DBEs are unrecoverable — candidates for extra scrutiny or RMA if growing.

panels.title: Reset Candidates (heuristic: heavy SBE + remapped rows)
panels.description: GPUs with 24h single-bit ECC growth >100 AND any correctable remapped rows. The canonical 'rows pending remap' signal is not yet collected, so this combination is a proxy.

targets.expr: (sum by (vm_name, gpu) (increase(DCGM_FI_DEV_ECC_SBE_VOL_TOTAL{cluster_id=~"$cluster"}[24h])) > 100) and on (vm_name, gpu) (sum by (vm_name, gpu) (DCGM_FI_DEV_CORRECTABLE_REMAPPED_ROWS{cluster_id=~"$cluster"}) > 0)

title: DCGM & Xid Errors
